Wireframes for trade-run milestone dialogs and the collapsible run/leg container that hosts them, styled to match Uplink's existing dark HUD overlay theme.

Uplink — trade run tracker

Milestone dialogs — draft v2

Revised after the first review pass. Every input below is still live — type in a field, flip a toggle, expand a leg — the totals, buttons, and header numbers react the way the real thing would. Reload this page to reset.

Changed from the first draft
  • Travel is now a real form — destination name plus a copy-to-clipboard button, for pasting straight into Mobiglass nav.
  • Loading type (Manual/Autoload) and the autoload fee moved into the Buy/Sell cargo dialogs themselves, as a toggle — not a separate step.
  • Confirm Loaded (buy side) is back to a single button — no fields, since the type/fee are already captured.
  • No separate confirm-unload step on the sale side at all — unloading has no real time cost in-game, so it's just the toggle+fee in Sell Cargo.
  • Added a live Investment / Projected Profit readout — this is the new Section A below.
  • The finalize/unsold-cargo branching flow is intentionally not designed yet — queued for once this flow and the ledger are working end to end.

Part A

Run & leg container

Answers "how do I go back and check what I bought this for" — the run collapses as a whole, and every leg collapses independently underneath it. A finalized leg stays inspectable (read-only) after the fact; this is also what a Ledger row becomes once the whole run is done — the same card, fully collapsed, fully locked. The header's Investment / Projected profit numbers are live against whichever leg is still open — try editing the Sell Cargo leg below.

Editing these updates the header above live.

Part B

Milestone dialogs, in detail

Each dialog below shown in isolation, in the order one leg walks through them — in the real app these render inline inside the leg's expanded row, the way the Sell Cargo example in Part A does above, not as a separate page.

01

Travel — depart & arrive

started_at, reached_at

Names the place the terminal actually sits at — terminal.city_name / outpost_name / space_station_name, whichever is set — not the terminal's own display name, since that's what you'd type into Mobiglass, not "Orison TDD."

The copy button puts just that name on the clipboard for pasting straight into the in-game nav search — the whole point is not needing to alt-tab out to look it up.

Writes → trade_leg.started_at or trade_leg.reached_at, timestamp only.
Fires from
BUY
Orison TDD
32 SCU Agricium @ 14 aUEC
Depart for pickup

↓ opens

Travel

Acquisition — depart for pickup

Travel to Orison
City on Crusader — Orison TDD terminal
02

Buy cargo

transaction_completed_at (acquisition)

Quantity and price start prefilled from the searched route but are corrected here to what actually happened — travel time means the market may have moved.

Loading type defaults from the route's known dock availability (has_loading_dock_origin) but stays editable — the fee field only appears once Autoload is picked, since Manual has none.

Writes → trade_leg.quantity_scu, price_per_unit, cargo_transfer_type, cargo_transfer_fee, transaction_completed_at.
Fires from

Orison TDD

03

Confirm loaded (buy side only)

transferred_at (acquisition)

No fields — type and fee were already captured in Buy Cargo above, this just confirms the physical load finished. Loading is the one direction that has a real in-game time cost, which is where a future load-timer feature would hook in for Autoload specifically; not built here, just leaving room for it.

There's no equivalent step on the sale side at all — see Sell Cargo below.

Writes → trade_leg.transferred_at, timestamp only.
Fires from
BUY
Orison TDD
32 SCU Agricium @ 14 aUEC
Confirm loaded

↓ opens

Confirm loaded

Manual — no fee recorded

04

Sell cargo

transaction_completed_at (sale) — also satisfies transferred_at

Mirrors Buy Cargo — quantity and price corrected to what actually happened, plus the same loading-type toggle. Unloading has no separate time cost in-game right now (auto or manual), so there's nothing to confirm afterward — this dialog alone covers both the sale and the transfer, which is why it also stamps transferred_at on submit.

Selling less than planned is common (a buy order dries up mid-sale) — the confirm button reads the quantity live rather than treating it as a fixed target.

Writes → trade_leg.quantity_scu, price_per_unit, cargo_transfer_type, cargo_transfer_fee, transaction_completed_at, transferred_at.

Resolved this round

  • The sale leg's quantity does not auto-sync when the buy leg is corrected — stays independent until edited at Sell Cargo.
  • Live Investment / Projected Profit added at the run level (Part A) — computed from actuals where known, planned estimates where not yet transacted.

Deferred, not forgotten

  • The finalize flow for unsold cargo (keep in inventory / retry here / redirect elsewhere) — picked back up once this dialog flow and the ledger are both working end to end, per your call.

Still open

  • Should Projected Profit turn red purely on sign (below 0), or also flag a milestone margin drop even while still positive — e.g. searched at 32%, now tracking toward 12%?
  • Confirm Loaded currently always needs a manual click, even for Autoload — is that right for v1, or should it matter that Autoload has a real quoted wait?
